name: Human Body Systems
description: Major organ systems of the human body -- their functions, key organs, and surprising facts

questions.question: What organ system includes the heart and blood vessels?
questions.explanation: The circulatory system pumps about 2,000 gallons of blood per day. If you laid all your blood vessels end to end, they would stretch over 60,000 miles -- enough to wrap around the Earth more than twice.

questions.question: What is the largest organ in the human body?
questions.explanation: An adult's skin covers about 22 square feet and weighs around 8 pounds. It is part of the integumentary system and completely replaces itself roughly every 27 days.

questions.question: How many bones are in the adult human body?
questions.explanation: Babies are born with about 270 bones, but many fuse together as they grow. Over a quarter of all your bones (54) are in your hands and feet alone.

questions.question: What organ system is responsible for gas exchange, bringing oxygen in and removing carbon dioxide?
questions.explanation: Your lungs contain about 300 million alveoli -- tiny air sacs where gas exchange happens. If you spread them flat, their total surface area would cover a tennis court.

questions.question: What organ in the digestive system is about 20 feet long in an adult, despite its misleading name?
questions.explanation: The small intestine is actually about 20 feet long -- much longer than the large intestine (about 5 feet). It is called 'small' because of its narrower diameter, not its length.

questions.question: What organ system includes the brain, spinal cord, and nerves?
questions.explanation: The human brain contains roughly 86 billion neurons. Nerve signals can travel at speeds up to 268 mph, which is why you can feel a stubbed toe almost instantly.

questions.question: What is the longest and strongest bone in the human body?
questions.explanation: The femur (thigh bone) can support up to 30 times the weight of an adult. It is part of the skeletal system and is roughly one-quarter of a person's total height.

questions.question: Approximately how many skeletal muscles are in the human body: about 200, 400, or 600?
questions.explanation: The human body has over 600 skeletal muscles, making up about 40% of total body weight. The gluteus maximus (your buttock muscle) is the largest, while the stapedius in your ear is the smallest.

questions.question: What pea-sized gland at the base of the brain is called the 'master gland' of the endocrine system?
questions.explanation: The pituitary gland is only about the size of a pea but controls growth, metabolism, and reproduction by signaling other glands. Despite its nickname, it actually takes orders from the hypothalamus above it.

questions.question: What organ of the lymphatic system filters blood, recycles old red blood cells, and holds a reserve of white blood cells?
questions.explanation: The spleen filters about a cup of blood every minute. You can survive without it, but you become more vulnerable to certain infections because it plays a key role in the immune response.

questions.question: What pair of organs in the urinary system filters waste from the blood to produce urine?
questions.explanation: Your kidneys filter all of your blood about 40 times per day, processing roughly 200 quarts. Each kidney contains about one million tiny filtering units called nephrons.

questions.question: What body system consists of skin, hair, nails, and sweat glands?
questions.explanation: The integumentary system is your body's first line of defense against the outside world. You shed about 1.5 million skin cells every hour -- most household dust is actually dead skin.

questions.question: What is the largest single cell in the human body?
questions.explanation: The human egg cell (ovum) is about 0.1 mm in diameter -- just barely visible to the naked eye. It is roughly 16 times wider than a sperm cell and about 10,000 times wider than a red blood cell.

questions.question: What organ produces hydrochloric acid strong enough to dissolve metal?
questions.explanation: Stomach acid has a pH of about 1.5 to 3.5 -- strong enough to dissolve a razor blade. The stomach protects itself by producing a new mucus lining every few days.

questions.question: What part of the nervous system controls involuntary actions like heart rate, digestion, and breathing?
questions.explanation: The autonomic nervous system works without conscious thought, keeping you alive while you sleep. It has two branches: sympathetic ('fight or flight') and parasympathetic ('rest and digest').
